name: MyImage prod - Main automation flow
description: Template version of the main CS automation flow. Reads project-specific config from the cs_project_config data table via project_key; all hardcoded values replaced with table references. Duplicate this workflow for new projects.

values.content:
You are a translation engine.
Your task is to translate user-provided text into English and identify the language of the original text.

Rules (must be followed strictly):
Translate exactly and literally
Do not add, remove, infer, or reinterpret any information
Do not explain, summarize, rephrase, or improve wording
Preserve tone, intent, ambiguity, and emotional content exactly
Preserve formatting, punctuation, emojis, and line breaks
Do not normalize slang, profanity, or errors
If a term cannot be translated precisely, transliterate it
If the input is already in English, return it unchanged
If the input is partially English, translate only the non-English parts

For originalTicketLanguage, output exactly one value from this list and nothing else:
english, spanish, portuguese, french, deutsch, nederlands, polski, turkish, italian, norsk, svenska, other_lang

Rules for originalTicketLanguage:
Use the code for the primary (dominant) language of the original text, ignoring incidental English words, names, or product terms
If the original text is already in English, output english
If the language is not one of the listed codes, output other_lang
If the language cannot be determined, output other_lang
Output the value exactly as written in the list, with no extra characters

Output a JSON object containing:
title: string // translated title
description: string // translated description
originalTicketLanguage: string // one value from the allowed list

Formatting:
Must be valid JSON
No commentary, labels, or notes
No markdown unless it exists in the input

You analyze customer support messages and classify them into exactly one of the following categories based only on the user’s written message

CATEGORIES:

Refunds (r)
Use this category if the customer is asking for money back, a refund, a charge reversal, payment withdrawal, or mentions being charged and wanting the charge undone, or asking to remove this payment.  
This includes refund requests even if the customer also requests cancelling or closing their account in addition to the refund.

Cancellations  (c)
Use this category if the customer is asking to unsubscribe, cancel, stop, pause, or end a subscription or account AND does NOT request money back. This category does NOT include account deletion requests

Other  (o)
Use this category for all other messages, including:
account deletion requests
product questions
feature requests
account access issues
complaints without a refund or cancellation request
unclear or ambiguous messages
harassment or threatening, in this case the “other” category is considered as refusing to classify the ticket

IMPORTANT RULES:
If a refund is requested or clearly implied, choose Refunds category
Do NOT assume a refund request from frustration or complaints alone.
Do NOT assume a refund from a cancellation request unless money back is explicitly requested.
If the intent is unclear or ambiguous, choose Other 
If the message contains forwarded content (often labelled with "Forwarded message") disregard everything that comes after that and only consider the content before it in the classification

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Output only one letter based on the category of the ticket, the output must be a single token
r = Refunds
c = Cancellations
o = Other

Do not explain your reasoning. Output only the letter.

parameters.name: ticket.comment.body
parameters.value: =Note: This ticket has been analysed by the CS automation and the confidence level was below the defined threshold in order to proceed. Please handle this request manually.

parameters.content:
# Template configuration

## How this template works
1. **Set project_key** holds the hardcoded key for this deployment.
2. **Get Project Config** fetches the row from the `cs_project_config` data table.
3. All downstream nodes reference values via `$('Get Project Config').first().json.column_name`.

## To duplicate for a new project
- Duplicate this workflow.
- Change the value in **Set project_key**.
- Add a new row in `cs_project_config` for that key.
- Update the Zendesk Trigger credentials and webhook ID.
- Note: the `ticket.ticket_field_*` in the Zendesk Trigger fields list is hardcoded (trigger params don't allow expressions). Update it to the correct field ID for the new project.
# Confidence level too low

## Status: open
## Category: none
## Assign to CS
# User doesn't exist - multiple

## Status: pending
## Category: pass and acc info
## Macro: acc details
# Payment provider not supported

## Status: open
## Category: none
## Assign to CS
# Fallback error catcher
# Other requests

## Status: open
## Category: none
## Assign to CS
# Deletion request

## Status: solved
## Category: Cancellation > account removal
## Macro: account deletion

values.content:
You classify customer support messages to determine whether the message contains an explicit account deletion request.

POSITIVE CASE — output true:
The customer must use clear, direct language requesting account deletion. Examples of qualifying phrases (similar phrases still apply):
"delete my account"
"I want my account deleted"
"please delete my account"
"delete my data and account"
"I'd like you to delete my account"

NEGATIVE CASE — output false — for ALL of the following:
Cancellation requests ("cancel my account", "cancel my subscription")
General frustration or complaints without explicit deletion language
Refund requests

STRICT RULES:

The word "delete" must appear explicitly in reference to the account
Cancellation alone does NOT qualify
Do NOT infer deletion intent from context, tone, or frustration
If the message is ambiguous or deletion is only implied, output false

OUTPUT FORMAT:
Respond only with valid JSON. No explanation.
{"is_delete_request": true}
or
{"is_delete_request": false}

You are an information extraction system for customer support messages.

Analyze the customer support message and extract the customer's email address if one is explicitly provided.

Return only a JSON object that matches the provided schema.

Rules:
- The output must contain exactly one key: customer_email.
- If the customer message contains a valid email address, set customer_email to that email address as a string.
- If the customer message does not contain a valid email address, set customer_email to "none".
- Do not include markdown, explanations, comments, or extra text.
- Do not guess, infer, reconstruct, or fabricate an email address.
- If multiple valid email addresses are present return "none"
- If the message contains forwarded content (often labelled with "Forwarded message") disregard everything that comes after that and only consider the content before it in the classification

parameters.value:
=Note: 
Customer's subscription status is INITIAL or INCOMPLETE, which means the user didn't complete the first payment. There's no subscription to cancel there, the subscription has not been created yet. The customer either abandoned payment process or failed to pay.

Please take over this request manually.

body.ticket.title: Refund for subscription
body.ticket.description:
----------------------------------------------

ala łyskawa, Jul 22, 2026, 15:38

Hello, I am writing to request a full refund for a charge of 229 PLN made to my account via **Apple Pay** on July 22, 2026.

I was not aware that I was signing up for a paid/recurring subscription. This charge was completely unexpected, and I have not used the paid features of the platform.

As a university student on a very tight budget, this amount is critical for my daily living expenses. I kindly ask for your understanding and request a full refund back to my original payment method - Apple Pay.

Could you also please ensure that any active subscription on my account is immediately canceled so no further charges occur? But I think I actually canceled it just a few minutes ago after the payment, but just to make sure.

Thank you very much for your time and assistance.

Best regards, Alicja Łyskawa.

 ![](https://myimage.zendesk.com/attachments/token/7e2v1M0VmO73o6GflpFxHzZup/?name=IMG_2788.png)
